extra.reasoning: The property requires that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never both true. rec_drug_a is set true only by r4, whose antecedent is a 12-way conjunction that includes prior_gi_bleed. rec_drug_b is set true only by r2, whose antecedent is prior_gi_bleed alone. Since r4's condition is a strict superset of r2's condition, any input that fires r4 (making rec_drug_a true) necessarily also satisfies prior_gi_bleed, firing r2 (making rec_drug_b true). No conflict resolution prevents this: r2 is the only rule targeting rec_drug_b and r4 is the only rule targeting rec_drug_a, so each output is set true with no competing rule to override it via priority. Setting all 12 conjuncts of r4 to true (prior_gi_bleed, g6pd_deficiency, hepatic_impairment, heart_failure, anticoagulated, hyperkalemia, low_ejection_fraction, ckd_dialysis, diabetes, qt_prolongation, asthma, seizure_history) yields rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion. peptic_ulcer and elderly only affect rec_drug_c (irrelevant) and are set false. The property is violated.
